title: CNBC's Jim Cramer Says He Needs 'Cold Hard' Proof AI Is Paying Off  - Slashdot
excerpt: In a sign of our times, CNBC's Jim Cramer "said Wednesday that it's time for companies to prove artificial intelligence is paying off," reports CNBC: "I need cold hard return facts," the "Mad Money" host said. "Or, I, too, will grow more skeptical than I am now...." While Cramer said he remains o...
content_html:
In a sign of our times, CNBC's Jim Cramer "said Wednesday that it's time for companies to prove artificial intelligence is paying off," reports CNBC: "I need cold hard return facts," the "Mad Money" host said. "Or, I, too, will grow more skeptical than I am now...." While Cramer said he remains optimistic about the long-term opportunity, he argued the market needs more evidence that those investments are translating into measurable financial returns for customers. Cramer said one of his biggest concerns this earnings season is that companies adopting AI have largely failed to point to meaningful revenue gains or cost savings from the technology. "We're still early in the earnings season but already we are not hearing anything material about the use of AI," he said...

While AI infrastructure companies continue to benefit from the spending boom, Cramer said the same cannot yet be said for many of the businesses buying the technology... Cramer said only a handful of companies, most notably fintech firm Block and web-security provider Cloudflare, have clearly attributed recent layoffs to AI adoption. Block did so in February, while Cloudflare's job cuts were disclosed in May. Plus, critics argue some companies may also cite AI as a buzzy excuse for cuts, leading to the creation of the term "AI washing." Ultimately, Cramer said that if more businesses do not begin reporting tangible returns, the AI skeptics will grow louder, with ramifications for the tech industry's big spenders.
content_text: In a sign of our times, CNBC's Jim Cramer "said Wednesday that it's time for companies to prove artificial intelligence is paying off," reports CNBC: "I need cold hard return facts," the "Mad Money" host said. "Or, I, too, will grow more skeptical than I am now...." While Cramer said he remains optimistic about the long-term opportunity, he argued the market needs more evidence that those investments are translating into measurable financial returns for customers. Cramer said one of his biggest concerns this earnings season is that companies adopting AI have largely failed to point to meaningful revenue gains or cost savings from the technology. "We're still early in the earnings season but already we are not hearing anything material about the use of AI," he said... While AI infrastructure companies continue to benefit from the spending boom, Cramer said the same cannot yet be said for many of the businesses buying the technology... Cramer said only a handful of companies, most notably fintech firm Block and web-security provider Cloudflare, have clearly attributed recent layoffs to AI adoption. Block did so in February, while Cloudflare's job cuts were disclosed in May. Plus, critics argue some companies may also cite AI as a buzzy excuse for cuts, leading to the creation of the term "AI washing." Ultimately, Cramer said that if more businesses do not begin reporting tangible returns, the AI skeptics will grow louder, with ramifications for the tech industry's big spenders.

blocks.html_en:
In a sign of our times, CNBC's Jim Cramer "said Wednesday that it's time for companies to prove artificial intelligence is paying off," reports CNBC: "I need cold hard return facts," the "Mad Money" host said. "Or, I, too, will grow more skeptical than I am now...." While Cramer said he remains optimistic about the long-term opportunity, he argued the market needs more evidence that those investments are translating into measurable financial returns for customers. Cramer said one of his biggest concerns this earnings season is that companies adopting AI have largely failed to point to meaningful revenue gains or cost savings from the technology. "We're still early in the earnings season but already we are not hearing anything material about the use of AI," he said...

While AI infrastructure companies continue to benefit from the spending boom, Cramer said the same cannot yet be said for many of the businesses buying the technology... Cramer said only a handful of companies, most notably fintech firm Block and web-security provider Cloudflare, have clearly attributed recent layoffs to AI adoption. Block did so in February, while Cloudflare's job cuts were disclosed in May. Plus, critics argue some companies may also cite AI as a buzzy excuse for cuts, leading to the creation of the term "AI washing." Ultimately, Cramer said that if more businesses do not begin reporting tangible returns, the AI skeptics will grow louder, with ramifications for the tech industry's big spenders.

sentences.en: In a sign of our times, CNBC's Jim Cramer "said Wednesday that it's time for companies to prove artificial intelligence is paying off," reports CNBC: "I need cold hard return facts," the "Mad Money" host said.
sentences.zh: 据 CNBC 报道，CNBC 的吉姆·克莱默 (Jim Cramer) 周三表示，现在是公司证明人工智能正在获得回报的时候了，这是我们这个时代的一个标志。这位《Mad Money》主持人表示，“我需要冷酷无情的回报事实”。

sentences.en: "Or, I, too, will grow more skeptical than I am now...."
sentences.zh: “或者说，我也会变得比现在更加怀疑……”

sentences.en: While Cramer said he remains optimistic about the long-term opportunity, he argued the market needs more evidence that those investments are translating into measurable financial returns for customers.
sentences.zh: 尽管克莱默表示他对长期机会仍然持乐观态度，但他认为市场需要更多证据来证明这些投资正在为客户转化为可衡量的财务回报。

sentences.en: Cramer said one of his biggest concerns this earnings season is that companies adopting AI have largely failed to point to meaningful revenue gains or cost savings from the technology.
sentences.zh: 克莱默表示，他本财报季最大的担忧之一是，采用人工智能的公司基本上未能指出该技术带来的有意义的收入增长或成本节省。

sentences.en: "We're still early in the earnings season but already we are not hearing anything material about the use of AI," he said...
sentences.zh: 他说：“我们仍处于财报季初期，但我们还没有听到任何有关人工智能使用的材料。”

sentences.en: While AI infrastructure companies continue to benefit from the spending boom, Cramer said the same cannot yet be said for many of the businesses buying the technology...
sentences.zh: 克莱默表示，虽然人工智能基础设施公司继续受益于支出热潮，但对于许多购买该技术的企业来说，情况却并非如此……

sentences.en: Cramer said only a handful of companies, most notably fintech firm Block and web-security provider Cloudflare, have clearly attributed recent layoffs to AI adoption.
sentences.zh: Cramer 表示，只有少数公司，尤其是金融科技公司 Block 和网络安全提供商 Cloudflare，明确地将最近的裁员归因于人工智能的采用。

sentences.en: Block did so in February, while Cloudflare's job cuts were disclosed in May.
sentences.zh: Block 在 2 月份这样做了，而 Cloudflare 的裁员则在 5 月份披露。

sentences.en: Plus, critics argue some companies may also cite AI as a buzzy excuse for cuts, leading to the creation of the term "AI washing."
sentences.zh: 此外，批评者认为，一些公司可能还会引用人工智能作为裁员的流行借口，从而导致“人工智能清洗”一词的产生。

sentences.en: Ultimately, Cramer said that if more businesses do not begin reporting tangible returns, the AI skeptics will grow louder, with ramifications for the tech industry's big spenders.
sentences.zh: 最后，克莱默表示，如果更多的企业不开始报告切实的回报，人工智能怀疑论者的声音将会更大，这会对科技行业的大笔支出产生影响。

blocks.html_en: While AI infrastructure companies continue to benefit from the spending boom, Cramer said the same cannot yet be said for many of the businesses buying the technology... Cramer said only a handful of companies, most notably fintech firm Block and web-security provider Cloudflare, have clearly attributed recent layoffs to AI adoption. Block did so in February, while Cloudflare's job cuts were disclosed in May. Plus, critics argue some companies may also cite AI as a buzzy excuse for cuts, leading to the creation of the term "AI washing." Ultimately, Cramer said that if more businesses do not begin reporting tangible returns, the AI skeptics will grow louder, with ramifications for the tech industry's big spenders.

sentences.zh: 克莱默表示，虽然人工智能基础设施公司继续受益于支出热潮，但对于许多购买该技术的企业来说，情况却并非如此……

sentences.en: Cramer said only a handful of companies, most notably fintech firm Block and web-security provider Cloudflare, have clearly attributed recent layoffs to AI adoption.
sentences.zh: Cramer 表示，只有少数公司，尤其是金融科技公司 Block 和网络安全提供商 Cloudflare，明确地将最近的裁员归因于人工智能的采用。

sentences.en: Block did so in February, while Cloudflare's job cuts were disclosed in May.
sentences.zh: Block 在 2 月份这样做了，而 Cloudflare 的裁员则在 5 月份披露。

sentences.en: Plus, critics argue some companies may also cite AI as a buzzy excuse for cuts, leading to the creation of the term "AI washing."
sentences.zh: 此外，批评者认为，一些公司可能还会引用人工智能作为裁员的流行借口，从而导致“人工智能清洗”一词的产生。

sentences.en: Ultimately, Cramer said that if more businesses do not begin reporting tangible returns, the AI skeptics will grow louder, with ramifications for the tech industry's big spenders.
sentences.zh: 最后，克莱默表示，如果更多的企业不开始报告切实的回报，人工智能怀疑论者的声音将会更大，这会对科技行业的大笔支出产生影响。

content_html_zh: 据 CNBC 报道，CNBC 的吉姆·克莱默 (Jim Cramer) 周三表示，现在是公司证明人工智能正在获得回报的时候了，这是我们这个时代的一个标志。这位《Mad Money》主持人表示，“我需要冷酷无情的回报事实”。 “或者说，我也会变得比现在更加怀疑……” 尽管克莱默表示他对长期机会仍然持乐观态度，但他认为市场需要更多证据来证明这些投资正在为客户转化为可衡量的财务回报。 克莱默表示，他本财报季最大的担忧之一是，采用人工智能的公司基本上未能指出该技术带来的有意义的收入增长或成本节省。 他说：“我们仍处于财报季初期，但我们还没有听到任何有关人工智能使用的材料。” 克莱默表示，虽然人工智能基础设施公司继续受益于支出热潮，但对于许多购买该技术的企业来说，情况却并非如此…… Cramer 表示，只有少数公司，尤其是金融科技公司 Block 和网络安全提供商 Cloudflare，明确地将最近的裁员归因于人工智能的采用。 Block 在 2 月份这样做了，而 Cloudflare 的裁员则在 5 月份披露。 此外，批评者认为，一些公司可能还会引用人工智能作为裁员的流行借口，从而导致“人工智能清洗”一词的产生。 最后，克莱默表示，如果更多的企业不开始报告切实的回报，人工智能怀疑论者的声音将会更大，这会对科技行业的大笔支出产生影响。
